seoTitle: Create Customer Statements in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Generate professional customer statements in QuickBooks Online to summarize open balances and remind clients of past-due invoices.
intro: When a client has multiple open invoices, a single statement is often clearer than a stack of separate bills. This guide shows how to generate, filter, and send customer statements in QuickBooks Online, including choosing the right statement type.

steps.heading: Open the customer directory
steps.body: Go to the Customers section and click the Batch actions dropdown or Create statements button.

steps.heading: Choose a statement type
steps.body: In the configuration panel, select Balance Forward, Open Item, or Transaction Statement depending on how you bill clients.

steps.heading: Set the date range
steps.body: Adjust the statement date and financial calendar range to capture the correct billing period.

steps.heading: Filter your client list
steps.body: Use the built-in filters to isolate accounts with open balances or specific past-due windows, unchecking any you want to exclude.

steps.heading: Preview and send
steps.body: Click Apply or Preview to review calculations, then select all validated statements and click Save and Send to deliver them.

faq.question: What's the difference between Balance Forward and Open Item statements?
faq.answer: Balance Forward shows a running total carried from prior periods, while Open Item lists each unpaid invoice individually; choose based on how detailed you want the statement to be.

faq.question: Can I exclude customers with a zero balance from statements?
faq.answer: Yes, the filter options let you isolate accounts with open balances so zero-balance customers aren't included.

faq.question: Can I send statements to multiple customers at once?
faq.answer: Yes, after filtering and selecting your client list, you can batch email statements in one send.

faq.question: Should I preview a statement before sending it?
faq.answer: Yes, previewing lets you verify invoice numbers, applied payments, and total balances line up correctly before it reaches the client.
